nodes.name: Time Complexity
nodes.definition:
It is a type of analysis to determine the approximate time required by an
algorithm to complete a given task when the number of inputs increases.
nodes.last_question_asked: If an algorithm has a time complexity of O(n) and you decide to optimize it to O(log n), how would you expect the execution time to change as the input size grows significantly, and what does this shift imply about the underlying design of the algorithm?

nodes.name: Big O notation
nodes.definition:
It describes worst case scenario of an algorithm to complete a task when
input size increases.
nodes.last_question_asked: If you have two different algorithms that both solve the same problem, why might an algorithm with a higher Big O complexity still be preferable to one with a lower complexity in a real-world computing environment?
